english: age (of a person, animal or plant, etc.)

etymology.description: 年(年) (nián, "year") refers to the passage of time in terms of years, marking the cycle of seasons. 齡(齡) (líng, "age") denotes the number of years a person, animal, or plant has lived, derived from the idea of teeth, which metaphorically reflects stages in life as teeth emerge in cycles across different age milestones.
etymology.interpretation: 年(年) (nián, "year") represents the passage of time as measured in increments of years, essential for counting the duration of life. 齡(齡) (líng, "age") uses the metaphor of teeth, which grow and change in well-defined stages, to denote life stages; thus, 'age' is the count of annual cycles a being has experienced. Combining these gives 年齡(年齡) (niánlíng, "age") as the measure of how many year-cycles a person, animal, or plant has traversed.

examples.chinese: 他的年龄比我大(他的年齡比我大)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de niánlíng bǐ wǒ dà.

examples.chinese: 在这个年龄(在這個年齡)，开始学习新东西是很重要的(開始學習新東西是很重要的)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhège niánlíng, kāishǐ xuéxí xīn dōngxī shì hěn zhòngyào de.
examples.english: At this age, it is important to start learning new things.

examples.chinese: 树的年龄可以通过年轮来判断(樹的年齡可以通過年輪來判斷)。
examples.pinyin: Shù de niánlíng kěyǐ tōngguò niánlún lái pànduàn.
examples.english: The age of a tree can be determined by its rings.
